claims.claim_text: MLX is a machine learning framework from Apple's machine learning research group, designed for Apple Silicon hardware.
claims.rationale: The MLX repository README directly describes MLX as an array framework for machine learning on Apple silicon and says it is brought to users by Apple machine learning research.

claims.claim_text: MLX provides Mac users with a practical local inference path for running open models on Apple Silicon.
claims.span_hint: section: What MLX Is | blockquote after introductory paragraph
claims.rationale: The MLX LM project directly documents text generation and fine-tuning of large language models on Apple silicon with MLX, which supports the module's local-inference claim.

further_reading.title: MLX
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary upstream source for what MLX is, how it is designed, and which Apple Silicon features it targets.

further_reading.title: MLX LM
further_reading.why_relevant: Shows the practical LLM inference workflow on Apple Silicon that learners would actually use.

further_reading.title: MLX Examples
further_reading.why_relevant: Provides concrete examples across text, image, audio, and multimodal workloads for learners exploring MLX capabilities.

notes: This module is mostly pedagogical and curriculum-framing prose; only the core MLX identity and local-inference capability claims clearly need inline citations.
